verses.text: For what profit is it, when some are so polluted, as not to be able to partake of the sacrifices?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that it did not save them at all; although they were zealous about the observances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But because they had not faith, even thus they profited nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In the next place he takes away the sacrifice from the type, and directs his discourse to the prototype, saying, “The bodies of those beasts whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the High Priest, are burned without the camp.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then those things were a type of these and thus Christ, suffering “without,” fulfilled all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here he makes it plain too that He suffered voluntarily, showing that those things were not accidental, but even the [Divine] arrangement itself was of a suffering “without.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: [He suffered] without, but His Blood was borne up into Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest then that we partake of Blood which has been carried into the Holy Place, the True Holy Place; of the Sacrifice of which the Priest alone had the privilege.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We therefore partake of the Truth [the Reality].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If then we partake not of “reproach” [only] but of sanctification, the “reproach” is the cause of the sanctification.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as He was reproached, so also are we.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If we go forth “without” therefore, we have fellowship with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what is, “Let us go forth to Him”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us have fellowship with Him in His sufferings; let us bear His reproach.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He did not simply bid us dwell “outside the gate,” but as He was reproached as a condemned person, so also we.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And “by Him let us offer a sacrifice to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of what kind of sacrifice does he speak?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “The fruit of lips giving thanks to His Name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: They [the Jews] brought sheep, and calves, and gave them to the Priest: let “us” bring none of these things, but thanksgiving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This “fruit” let “our lips” put forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For with such sacrifices God is well pleased.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us give such a sacrifice to Him, that He may offer [it] to The Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For in no other way it is offered except through the Son, or rather also through a contrite mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All these things [are said] for the weak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that the thanks belong to the Son is evident: since otherwise, how is the honor equal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “That all men” (He says) “should honor the Son even as they honor the Father.” ( John v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherein is the honor equal?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “The fruit of our lips giving thanks to His Name.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us bear all things thankfully, be it poverty, be it disease, be it anything else whatever: for He alone knows the things expedient for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For we know not what we should pray for as we ought.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We then who do not know even how to ask for what is fitting, unless we have received of the Spirit, let us take care to offer up thanksgiving for all things, and let us bear all things nobly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Are we in poverty?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us give thanks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Are we in sickness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us give thanks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Are we falsely accused?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us give thanks: when we suffer affliction, let us give thanks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This brings us near to God: then we even have God for our debtor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But when we are in prosperity, it is we who are debtors and liable to be called to account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when we are in prosperity, we are debtors to God: and oftentimes these things bring a judgment upon us, while those are for a payment of sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Those [afflictions] draw down mercy, they draw down kindness: while these on the other hand lift up even to an insane pride, and lead also to slothfulness, and dispose a man to fancy great things concerning himself; they puff up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore the prophet also said, “It is good for me, Lord, that Thou hast afflicted me; that I may learn Thy statutes.” ( Ps. cxix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When Hezekiah had received blessings and been freed from calamities, his heart was lifted up on high; when he fell sick, then was he humbled, then he became near to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “When He slew them,” it says, “then they sought Him diligently, and turned, and were early in coming to God.” ( Ps. lxxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “When the beloved waxed gross and fat, then he kicked.” ( Deut. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “the Lord is known when He executeth judgments.” ( Ps. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Affliction is a great good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Narrow is the way” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ), so that affliction thrusts us into the narrow [way].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He who is not pressed by affliction cannot enter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he who afflicts himself in the narrow [way], is he who also enjoys ease; but he that spreads himself out, does not enter in, and suffers from being so to say wedged in.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: See how Paul enters into this narrow way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He “keeps under” his “body” ( 1 Cor. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 27 ), so as to be able to enter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore, in all his afflictions, he continued giving thanks unto God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hast thou lost thy property?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This hath lightened thee of the most of thy wideness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hast thou fallen from glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is another sort of wideness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hast thou been falsely accused?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Have the things said against thee, of which thou art nowise conscious to thyself been believed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Rejoice and leap for joy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “blessed are ye” (He says) “when men reproach you, and say all manner of evil against you, falsely, for My sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Rejoice and be exceeding glad, for great is your reward in Heaven.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou marvel, if thou art grieved, and wish to be set free from temptations?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Paul wished to be set free, and oftentimes entreated God, and did not obtain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the “thrice for this I besought the Lord,” is oftentimes; “and He said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee, for My strength is made perfect in weakness.” ( 2 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By “weakness,” he here means “afflictions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When he heard this he received it thankfully, and says, “Wherefore I take pleasure in infirmities” ( 2 Cor. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 10 ); that is, I am pleased, I rest in my afflictions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For all things then let us give thanks, both for comfort, and for affliction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not murmur: let us not be unthankful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Naked came I out of my mother’s womb, naked also shall I depart.” ( Job i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou didst not come forth glorious, do not seek glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou wast brought into life naked, not of money alone, but also of glory, and of honorable name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Consider how great evils have oftentimes arisen from wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “It is easier” (it is said) “for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of Heaven.” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou to how many good things wealth is a hindrance, and dost thou seek to be rich?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou not rejoice that the hindrance has been overthrown?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So narrow is the way which leadeth into the Kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So broad is wealth, and full of bulk and swelling out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore He says, “Sell that thou hast” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 21 ), that that way may receive thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou yearn after wealth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this cause He took it away from thee, that He might free thee from slavery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For true fathers also, when a son is corrupted by some mistress, and having given him much exhortation they do not persuade him to part from her, send the mistress into banishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Such also is abundance of wealth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because the Lord cares for us, and delivers us from the harm [which arises] therefrom, He takes away wealth from us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not then think poverty an evil: sin is the only evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For neither is wealth a good thing by itself: to be well-pleasing to God is the only good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Poverty then let us seek, this let us pursue: so shall we lay hold on heaven, so shall we attain to the other good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Which may we all attain by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost be glory, power, honor, now and ever and world without end.
